# Transcrição: That_time_I_froze_on_stage

- **Fonte:** That_time_I_froze_on_stage
- **Duração:** 01:18
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:18]

When I first started my speaking career, I had an incredible event to speak at. It was ICMI's showcase. So this is where they showcase Australia's speakers. And if you do a good job there, you get booked. I rehearsed my thing so much that it was really natural. But because I was panicking and I was freaking out like, man, I was freaking out inside. I walked out mental blank. And the silence got really awkward. Because I just, I lost what I was going to do. And in those moments, I believe it's so important to allow yourself to be vulnerable and say it. Like, it's so powerful because it's humanising. And then I don't know what led me to that, but I just said, I'm really nervous and got a laugh. And I go, I've been preparing for this so much. I've been so focused on impressing every single one of you here that I totally forgot how I'm meant to start my presentation. Can I walk out again? And everyone was like, and everyone clapped the hell out of that for me. And my career actually propelled. The audience loves humanisation. So by acknowledging it, I came out and did it again. It really changed my perception. Because every other speaker was so polished, I had a mistake. It made me human. So I think in moments like that, dare to be vulnerable. It's a power that we don't tap into enough.
